scope: S4096 DeepSeek-V4 sparse-MLA backward source-boundary kernel direction on MI350

measured_s4096_rocwmma_score_page_scaffo.read: Changing the existing rocWMMA score-page scaffold from serial token chunks to token-parallel CTAs does not make the score producer viable. Score-only remains above the retained full Triton sparse-attention backward boundary, so adding a local dKV epilogue around this producer would not materialize a useful latency candidate.

rejected_branches.name: local_epilogue_around_current_rocwmma_score_page
rejected_branches.reason: The producer alone is 15.39-16.40 ms at S4096, already slower than the 11.13 ms full sparse-attention backward gate.

rejected_branches.name: direct_source_ck_score_producer_swap
rejected_branches.reason: CK score-only direct-source loop measured about 32.70 ms, worse than rocWMMA score-only.

rejected_branches.name: late_dkv_only_tilelang_or_reducer_replacement
rejected_branches.reason: It arrives after PyTorch/Triton has already materialized or bridged expensive score/prob/selected-state work, so isolated dKV wins do not materialize e2e.

next_kernel_contract.name: persistent_or_cooperative_mfma_sparse_mla_backward_v0
next_kernel_contract.goal: Consume score/prob/scaled-dP inside the same producer-consumer ownership schedule before HBM, then perform group-aware dKV reduction before global writes.
producer_requirements: Keep query-major reuse of q and grad_output while sweeping selected compressed groups.
Stage selected KV and q/grad_output operands for MFMA instead of direct-source scalar or direct-source CK loops.
Compute QK and dO.V score pages into registers or LDS ring-buffer pages.
Immediately form prob and scaled-dP from lse/output row terms.
Feed dQ and dKV consumers from the live score page without an HBM score/prob slab.
consumer_requirements: Compute dQ from scaled-dP and selected KV within the same ownership schedule.
Compute dKV contributions from scaled-dP*q plus prob*grad_output.
Use route-aware local segmented reduction by compressed group before any global write.
Emit either reduced global atomics or compact reduced rows only after local grouping.
next_kernel_contract.resource_warning: A single ordinary CTA cannot hold a T32/SB512/full-D group accumulator in LDS. The implementation needs a rolling D tile, a tiny score ring buffer consumed immediately, and either persistent group ownership or a compact reduced partial stream. The current rocWMMA scaffold does not provide enough control or producer speed.

process_pipeline.sasg_basic_h: CubeCompute preloads Cube12, signals Vec, waits on the opposite ping-pong buffer, then runs Cube345. VecCompute waits for Cube12, runs vector softmax/grad processing, and signals the next cube stage.
process_pipeline.amd_lesson: A useful AMD analogue needs explicit producer-consumer ownership and ping-pong/ring-buffer staging. A normal single CTA score loop is not equivalent to Ascend's AIC/AIV overlap.

cube4.source_detail: cube4Process writes mm4ResWorkspaceGm by D loops after loading dS and query into local cube buffers.
cube4.amd_mapping: MFMA dKV-key contribution producer: scaled_dP^T @ q, preferably consumed before an unreduced selected-row HBM slab.

cube5.source_detail: cube5Process writes mm5ResWorkspaceGm by D loops after loading P and dY into local cube buffers.
cube5.amd_mapping: MFMA dKV-value contribution producer: prob^T @ grad_output, fused with the dk_partial stream before final write.

scatter.source_detail: ScatterAdd reads mm4ResWorkspaceGm and mm5ResWorkspaceGm into UB, scales dk, adds dv, and uses SetAtomicAdd<float>() before copying into original/compressed KV gradient.
scatter.amd_lesson: Ascend does not eliminate scatter-add; it moves it behind fused Cube/Vector workspaces and controls the pipeline. AMD should similarly avoid framework-visible P/dP/selected-KV slabs, then reduce or compact before global atomics.

score_producer_only_s4096_ms.reason: The old sparse-MLA forward/backward envelope leaves only a few ms for score recompute if full backward is to approach 5-11 ms.

full_source_boundary_dq_dkv_s4096_ms.reason: Must beat the retained Triton sparse-attention backward boundary before e2e TFLOPs can plausibly improve.

materialized_e2e.when: Only after the S4096 source-boundary latency gate improves.
materialized_e2e.shape: 8xMI350 TP1 PP1 CP1 FSDP8 EP8 MBS1 S4096 GBS128
materialized_e2e.metric: sustained late-step TFLOPs/GPU and peak memory under /scratch

decision: Do not spend the next kernel iteration on a dKV reducer bolted onto source_boundary_score_page_rocwmma_d512.hip. Start a lower-level HIP/rocWMMA or CK/MFMA producer-consumer kernel where the score tile and dKV grouped epilogue are designed together.
